sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The evidence shows a significant increase in orders, indicating accelerating demand.

evidence.text: Total backlog was $5,088 million at December 31, 2023 and $3,605 million at December 31, 2022, an increase of 41.1%, with backlog from the acquisition of Evoqua contributing $1,268 million, or 35.2%, of the increase.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: The backlog increased by 41.1%, a substantial growth driven by the Evoqua acquisition.

factors.summary: The company is successfully raising prices.

evidence.text: Gross margin decline for the year included 60 basis points from increases in acquired intangible asset amortization and special charges as compared to 2022. Additionally, the gross margin decline for the year included 530 basis points of negative operating impacts, driven by 230 basis points of inflation, 140 basis points of unfavorable impacts from the Evoqua acquisition, 80 basis points of unfavorable mix, and 30 basis points of increased spending on strategic investments. These impacts were partially offset by favorable impacts of 510 basis points, driven by 270 basis points of price realization and 210 basis points of productivity savings.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Price realization contributed 270 basis points to offsetting negative impacts, showing successful price increases.

evidence.text: Gross margin decline for the year included 60 basis points from increases in acquired intangible asset amortization and special charges as compared to 2022. Additionally, the gross margin decline for the year included 530 basis points of negative operating impacts, driven by 230 basis points of inflation, 140 basis points of unfavorable impacts from the Evoqua acquisition, 80 basis points of unfavorable mix, and 30 basis points of increased spending on strategic investments.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Evidence indicates an unfavorable mix impact of 80 basis points, suggesting a negative shift in revenue composition.

evidence.text: The gross margin decline for the year included 60 basis points from increases in acquired intangible asset amortization and special charges as compared to 2022. Additionally, the gross margin decline for the year included 530 basis points of negative operating impacts, driven by 230 basis points of inflation, 140 basis points of unfavorable impacts from the Evoqua acquisition, 80 basis points of unfavorable mix, and 30 basis points of increased spending on strategic investments.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Inflation and other unfavorable impacts led to a 230 basis point increase, indicating rising input costs.

evidence.text: Gross margin as a percentage of consolidated revenue decreased 80 basis points to 36.9% in 2023 as compared to 37.7% in 2022.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Gross margin decreased by 80 basis points, showing a clear compression in margins.

evidence.text: Restructuring charges were $72 million in 2023 as compared to $15 million in 2022. During 2023, we incurred these charges primarily as a result of our acquisition of Evoqua.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Restructuring charges and productivity savings indicate cost actions are delivering some savings, though with associated costs.

evidence.text: These impacts were partially offset by favorable impacts of 510 basis points, driven by 270 basis points of price realization and 210 basis points of productivity savings.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Evidence shows 210 basis points of productivity savings, indicating improving productivity.

evidence.text: Backlog includes orders on hand as well as contractual customer agreements at the end of the period. Delivery schedules vary from customer to customer based on their requirements. Annual or multi-year contracts are subject to rescheduling and cancellation by customers due to the long-term nature of the contracts. As such, beginning total backlog, plus orders, minus revenues, will not equal ending total backlog due to contract adjustments, foreign currency fluctuations, and other factors. Typically, large projects require longer lead production cycles and deployment schedules and delays occur from time to time. Total backlog was $5,088 million at December 31, 2023 and $3,605 million at December 31, 2022, an increase of 41.1%, with backlog from the acquisition of Evoqua contributing $1,268 million, or 35.2%, of the increase.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The increase in backlog due to acquisitions indicates value creation.

factors.summary: Foreign exchange movements are having an impact.

evidence.text: Foreign currency translation (a) (17) (0.9) % (18) (1.0) % 1 (0.1) % (7) (0.9) % (41) (0.7) % Total change in orders 397 (20.7) % (24) (1.3) % (66) (3.8) % 937 (115.5) % 1,244 (19.9) % 2023 Orders $ 2,313 $ 1,770 $ 1,670 $ 1,748 $ 7,501 (a) Foreign currency translation impact for the year primarily due to the weakening in value of various currencies against the U.S. Dollar, the largest being the Chinese Yuan, the Canadian Dollar and the Norwegian Krone.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Foreign currency translation has caused a significant decrease in orders.
